text.title: Perplexity AI for Research: Method
text.summary: Perplexity Pro Search workflow for cited multi-source research synthesis, with query strategy by research type and key limitations.

section.title: What Perplexity Provides
section.p: Perplexity Pro Search retrieves and synthesizes results from multiple live web sources simultaneously, returning citations alongside the synthesized answer. Addresses Claude's knowledge cutoff limitation for market data, funding rounds, and recent events.

list.item: 6.4% of AI search market; 15% of Fortune 100 companies use for internal research
sonar-pro model recommended for research; sonar model for high-volume fact checks
Market sizing: "TAM for [market] 2025 with sources" — forces recency and citation
Competitor analysis: "[Company] recent news funding features 2025"

rule.statement: Use search_recency_filter: "year" in API calls to avoid stale data; Perplexity defaults to broader time windows that include outdated figures.
rule.rationale: Market data from 2022 presented as current can be off by a factor of 2-3x. Recency filtering is not the default; it must be set explicitly.

rule.statement: Formulate one atomic fact per query; compound queries ("market size AND competitors AND trends") produce diluted answers across all three sub-questions.
rule.rationale: Pro Search distributes its retrieval budget across all parts of a compound query. Atomic queries get the full retrieval budget for one fact.

list.item: Citations are URL-level, not page-level; a cited source may support only part of the synthesized claim
Perplexity's synthesis can merge facts from different time periods without flagging the temporal mismatch
Paywalled sources appear in citation lists but Perplexity cannot read behind the paywall
For rapidly changing data (live stats, breaking news), Perplexity's index lags real-time by hours to days
Only searches the public web; cannot access PDFs, internal databases, or proprietary data

rule.statement: Cross-validate high-stakes numbers (TAM, funding rounds, revenue) against at least one additional independent source (AlphaSense, Crunchbase, official press releases).
rule.rationale: Perplexity's synthesis sounds authoritative even when it cites a single low-quality source. Citation count and source quality must be checked, not just the answer text.

text.reference: Python batch Pro Search via REST API with citation extraction
